subject: Computer Networking
title: Software-Defined Networking
description: A fast breakdown of Software-Defined Networking for builders.

hook.text: Configuring routers one by one doesn't scale. What if you could program the entire network from a single screen?

buildup.text: SDN separates the control plane from the data plane. A central controller makes decisions; switches just forward.

discovery.text: Network engineers write policies in software. Changes deploy instantly across thousands of devices.

twist.text: Google adopted SDN early, managing its global backbone traffic far more efficiently than traditional routing.

climax.text: SDN powers modern cloud networks, data centers, and wide-area networks at companies like Microsoft and Facebook.

punchline.text: SDN made the network programmable. Code replaces cables.

quiz.question: What does SDN separate?
options: The control plane from the data plane
